description: Lock advertising_mass_media_early to mass-periodical and retail advertising that scales paid newspaper advertising rather than requiring photography.

checks.reason: The Smithsonian chronology places newspaper advertising agents in the 1830s and 1840s; the 1840s decade also follows the graph's source-checked mass-newspaper milestone.
Paid public newspaper advertising is the narrower direct practice that mass advertising scales through larger periodical and retail channels.
The corrected scope keeps paid newspaper advertising as a direct lineage prerequisite.
Mass newspapers and magazines provide the distribution medium for large-audience advertising.
Mass-periodical distribution remains a direct enabling substrate for the node.
Photography can improve later advertising imagery but is not a direct prerequisite for early mass newspaper, magazine, poster, brand, and retail campaigns.
